sections.paragraph:
ἀνθρωπίνως adv. (s. ἀνθρώπινος; Thu.; Andoc. 1, 57; 2, 6 et al.; PSI XII, 1248, 17 [III a.d.]; TestSol 10:3 C; Jos., Ant. 19, 4; Tat. 4:1) as a human being θεοῦ ἀ. φανερουμένου when God appeared in human form IEph 19:3.—M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Andoc Andoc , V–IVb.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 80
